home.title: clawdboard -- AI Coding Usage Leaderboard

leaderboard.heading: AI Coding Usage Leaderboard
leaderboard.seoIntro: The community leaderboard for AI coding tools — Claude Code, OpenCode & Codex CLI. Track your spending, compare usage across developers, and see who's shipping the most with AI-powered coding. Free to join — sync your stats in one command.
leaderboard.heroHeadline: The #1 vibe coder spent {topCost} this week. Where do you rank?
leaderboard.heroDescription: Track your Claude Code, OpenCode & Codex CLI usage. Compare costs, streaks, and model breakdowns with developers worldwide. Free and open-source.
leaderboard.heroPrivacy: We never see your code — only aggregate token counts from local logs.
leaderboard.shareLeaderboardText: Someone spent {topCost} on AI coding this week. See who's at the top of the vibecoding leaderboard
leaderboard.cookingTooltip: Set your cooking link in Settings to show what you're building
leaderboard.getStarted: Run <command>npx clawdboard auth</command> to get started

syncBanner.message: Hey {username}, your stats aren't on the board yet. Run
syncBanner.cta: in your terminal to get ranked.

onboarding.connectDescription: Run a single command to link your GitHub, sync your usage, and set up automatic syncing:
onboarding.privacyNote: Your usage will sync automatically every 2 hours. Only numbers are shared — never your prompts, code, or project names.
onboarding.afterSync: Once synced, you'll be redirected to your profile on the leaderboard.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Everything you need to know about clawdboard, AI coding tool usage tracking, and how the leaderboard works.
faq.a1: clawdboard is a leaderboard that ranks developers by their AI coding tool usage. It supports Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex CLI. It shows estimated costs, token counts, daily activity streaks, and model breakdowns -- giving you and your team visibility into how much you're actually using AI coding tools.
faq.a2: Yes. clawdboard is completely free to use. There are no paid tiers, no ads, and no data monetization.
faq.a3: Any developer using AI coding tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, or Codex CLI. Whether you're curious about your own usage, want to benchmark against peers, or are a team lead tracking adoption -- clawdboard gives you the numbers.
faq.q4: How do I join the leaderboard?
faq.a4: Sign in with your GitHub account, then follow the setup guide in your dashboard. You'll install ccusage (a local CLI tool), run a single sync command, and your data appears on the leaderboard within seconds.
faq.a5: ccusage is the companion CLI that reads local log files from supported AI coding tools (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI) on your machine, extracts aggregate metrics (tokens and costs), and sends them to clawdboard. It never reads your code, project names, or conversation content.
faq.a6: Only aggregate AI coding metrics -- input, output, cache-creation, and cache-read tokens per day, the estimated cost at API pricing, and which models you used (Sonnet, Opus, Haiku, GPT-4o, etc.).
faq.a7: It does not track any code, file paths, project names, git branches, session IDs, conversation content, or anything from claude.ai web chat. It also does not track API tokens used by your own applications.
faq.a8: The CLI enforces a strict privacy allowlist -- only aggregate token counts and costs leave your machine. No code, prompts, or project context is ever transmitted. Your profile on clawdboard shows only the metrics you've synced.
faq.q9: Where does the data come from?
faq.a9: ccusage reads local log files that AI coding tools store on your machine (e.g., Claude Code in ~/.claude/, OpenCode and Codex in their respective directories). No API calls are made -- everything is parsed from your local logs.
faq.a10: It's the estimated cost of your token usage at published API rates (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.). This is a calculated estimate, not an actual bill. Think of it as a proxy for how intensively you're using AI coding tools.
faq.a11: A streak counts consecutive days where you used any supported AI coding tool. If you use it on Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday, that's a 3-day streak. Missing a day resets it. The leaderboard shows your current active streak.
faq.a12: By default, users are ranked by estimated cost (highest first). You can also sort by total tokens, number of active days, or current streak. Each column is sortable, and you can filter by time period (7d, 30d, this month, YTD, or a custom date range).
faq.q13: Does clawdboard track API usage from my own apps?
faq.a13: No. Only usage from supported AI coding tools (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI) is tracked. If you call APIs directly from your own code, that usage is completely separate and not included in clawdboard.
faq.a14: All models available through supported AI coding tools -- including Claude's Sonnet, Opus, and Haiku, as well as OpenAI's GPT-4o and o-series models. Your profile page shows a detailed breakdown of which models you've used and how much.
faq.a15: It's a Claude Code plugin that adds /sync, /stats, and /leaderboard slash commands directly inside Claude Code. It also auto-syncs your usage every 2 hours via a background hook -- so your stats stay up to date without manual effort.
faq.q16: How do I install the plugin?
faq.a16: Install it from GitHub by running "claude plugin add --from https://github.com/jim540/clawdboard-plugin" in your terminal. You'll need a free clawdboard account first -- sign in at clawdboard.ai with GitHub, then run "npx clawdboard auth" to link your machine.

contribute.heading: Contribute
contribute.intro: clawdboard is open-source. Here's how you can help.
contribute.repoDescription: Star the repo, browse the code, or fork it to get started.
contribute.bugReportsDescription: Found something broken? Open an issue with steps to reproduce and we'll look into it.
contribute.featureRequestsDescription: Have an idea for a new feature or improvement? We'd love to hear it.
contribute.pullRequestsDescription: Fix a bug, add a feature, or improve the docs. Check the contributing guide for setup instructions.
contribute.translationsDescription: clawdboard supports English, German, French, and Spanish. Help improve translations or add new languages.
contribute.quickStartNote: No secrets needed. Uses Docker for local Postgres, dev auth for sign-in.

statsPage.subtitle: usage statistics
statsPage.heroDescription: Real-time aggregate data from {totalUsers} developers who track their AI coding usage on clawdboard. All cost figures are estimates based on published API token pricing — not actual bills. Data covers all usage since January 2024.
statsPage.overviewDescription: Aggregate AI coding usage across all {totalUsers} registered developers since January 2024.
statsPage.trendsDescription: How much are developers spending on AI coding each day? This chart shows the 7-day moving average of estimated daily cost and active user count over the last {trendDays} days. Spikes often correspond to new model releases or major tool updates.
statsPage.modelsHeading: AI Model Popularity by Cost Share
statsPage.modelsDescription: Which AI models do developers actually use for coding? This breakdown shows estimated cost and token consumption per model across all users — including Claude (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku), OpenAI (GPT-4o, o-series), and others. Cost share reflects how much of total community spend goes to each model — higher-tier models cost more per token, so they can dominate spend even with fewer users. Click any model for detailed usage statistics.
statsPage.sourceHeading: Usage by Tool: Claude Code vs OpenCode vs Codex
statsPage.sourceDescription: Where does the data come from? This breakdown shows estimated cost share across the three supported AI coding tools. Each tool reads local usage logs and contributes to the aggregate statistics.
statsPage.growthDescription: How fast is the AI coding developer community growing? This chart tracks cumulative registrations on clawdboard by week. Growth accelerates around major model releases, new tool launches, and developer announcements.
statsPage.costAnalysisHeading: How Much Does AI Coding Actually Cost?
statsPage.costAnalysisP1: Based on data from {totalUsers} developers, the average AI coding user has an estimated all-time usage of {avgCost} in API-equivalent cost. The median is {medianCost}, reflecting the wide gap between casual users and power users who run AI coding tools daily for extended sessions.
statsPage.costAnalysisP2: These are not actual bills. They represent what the same token usage would cost at Anthropic's published API rates. Most AI coding tool users pay flat monthly subscriptions rather than per-token billing. The estimated cost is useful for comparing relative usage intensity across developers and understanding which models consume the most resources.
statsPage.costAnalysisP3: The community has logged {totalActiveDays} active days of AI coding usage, with the longest consecutive streak reaching {longestStreak} days. The busiest single day across the community saw {biggestDay} in estimated usage
statsPage.costAnalysisP4: Want to see where you stand? <leaderboard>View the leaderboard</leaderboard> to compare your usage, or <faq>read the FAQ</faq> to learn how tracking works.
statsPage.methodologyP1: All data on this page comes from developers who voluntarily track their AI coding usage through <link>clawdboard</link>. The <cli>clawdboard CLI</cli> reads local log files from supported tools — Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex CLI — on each developer's machine. It extracts aggregate token counts (input, output, cache creation, cache read) and the model used for each session.
statsPage.methodologyCostEstimationText: Token counts are multiplied by published API rates (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) for each model at the time of the session. This gives the API-equivalent cost — useful for comparison, but not what subscription users actually pay.
statsPage.methodologyPrivacyText: No code, prompts, file paths, project names, or conversation content is ever collected. Only aggregate token counts and model identifiers leave the developer's machine. See our <link>privacy policy</link> for details.
statsPage.methodologyLimitationsText: This is a self-selected sample of {totalUsers} developers, likely skewing toward heavier users and early adopters. It should not be interpreted as representative of all AI coding tool users.
statsPage.apiDescription: Need this data programmatically? clawdboard provides free, public API endpoints for aggregate usage statistics:
statsPage.apiExplanation: Both APIs return JSON with no authentication required. Rate-limited to 15 requests per minute. The stats endpoint supports the same period filters as the leaderboard: <today>today</today>, <sevenD>7d</sevenD>, <thirtyD>30d</thirtyD>, <thisMonth>this-month</thisMonth>, <ytd>ytd</ytd>, and <custom>custom</custom> (with <from>from</from> and <to>to</to> dates). Omit the period parameter for all-time aggregates. If you use this data, please cite clawdboard as the source.
statsPage.faqHeading: Frequently Asked Questions About AI Coding Usage
statsPage.faqDescription: Common questions about AI coding costs, model usage, and how this data is collected.
statsPage.faqQ1: How much does AI coding cost per month?
statsPage.faqA1: Based on data from {totalUsers} developers on clawdboard, the average estimated AI coding usage cost is {avgCost} total (not per month). The median is {medianCost}, meaning half of developers spend less than that. These are estimated API-equivalent costs — most developers pay flat monthly fees through provider subscriptions, not per-token billing.
statsPage.faqQ2: What are the most popular AI models for coding?
statsPage.faqA2: Model popularity varies over time as providers release new versions. Check the Model Popularity chart above for the latest breakdown by cost share and user count. Among Claude models, Sonnet tends to see the highest volume due to its speed-to-quality ratio, while Opus accounts for a significant share of total spend due to higher per-token pricing. OpenAI models like GPT-4o and o-series are also tracked for Codex CLI and OpenCode users.
statsPage.faqQ3: Where does this usage data come from?
statsPage.faqA3: Every data point comes from developers who voluntarily track their AI coding usage through clawdboard. The clawdboard CLI reads local log files from supported tools (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI) on each developer's machine, extracts aggregate token counts and cost estimates, and syncs them. No code, prompts, project names, or conversation content is ever collected — only token counts and estimated costs.
statsPage.faqQ4: How accurate are the cost estimates?
statsPage.faqA4: Cost estimates are calculated by multiplying token counts (input, output, cache creation, cache read) by published API rates for each model (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.). They represent the equivalent API cost — not an actual bill. Since most developers use AI coding tools through subscriptions with flat monthly pricing, the actual amount paid is typically lower than the estimated API-equivalent cost shown here.
statsPage.faqQ5: How often is this data updated?
statsPage.faqA5: Individual developers sync their usage every 2 hours by default. The aggregate statistics on this page are recalculated hourly. The data covers all usage since January 2024.
statsPage.faqQ6: What is a streak and how is it calculated?
statsPage.faqA6: A streak counts consecutive calendar days where a developer used AI coding tools at least once. Missing a single day resets the streak. The longest active streak in the community is currently {longestStreak} days. You can see individual streaks on the leaderboard and profile pages.
statsPage.faqQ7: Can I access this data programmatically?
statsPage.faqA7: Yes. clawdboard provides a free public API at {statsUrl} that returns community-wide aggregate statistics including total spend, token counts, model breakdowns, and methodology notes. The API is rate-limited to 15 requests per minute and returns JSON. The leaderboard API at {leaderboardUrl} is also public.
statsPage.faqQ8: Is this data representative of all AI coding tool users?
statsPage.faqA8: No. This is a self-selected sample of developers who choose to track and share their usage on clawdboard. It likely skews toward heavier users and early adopters. It should not be interpreted as representative of all AI coding tool users, but it does provide the largest public dataset of real AI coding usage patterns available.
statsPage.ctaHeading: Track Your Own AI Coding Usage
statsPage.ctaDescription: Join {totalUsers} developers on the leaderboard. Free, open-source, takes 30 seconds to set up.

statsTools.subtitle: tool comparison
statsTools.heroDescription: Side-by-side comparison of {toolList} usage from {totalUsers} developers on clawdboard. All costs are estimated from token counts and published API pricing.
statsTools.totalsDescription: Aggregate usage across all tracked AI coding tools.
statsTools.toolsTrackedSub: {count, plural, one {AI coding tool} other {AI coding tools}}
statsTools.costShareDescription: How is AI coding spend distributed across tools?
statsTools.toolBreakdownDescription: Usage, cost, and adoption metrics for each tracked tool.
statsTools.trendsDescription: How does daily estimated spend compare across {toolList}? This chart shows the 7-day moving average to smooth out daily variance.
statsTools.modelBreakdownDescription: Which models do {toolName} users prefer? Cost share breakdown across all models used through {toolName}.
statsTools.analysisP1Start: The AI coding tool ecosystem is evolving rapidly. clawdboard currently tracks {toolCount} {toolCount, plural, one {tool} other {tools}}:
statsTools.analysisCostRanking: By estimated cost, {leadingTool} currently leads at {leadingShare}% of total spend, followed by
statsTools.analysisCostExplanation: Cost share is influenced by both adoption (number of users) and model pricing (higher-tier models cost more per token). A tool with fewer users but more expensive models can have a larger cost share than a widely-adopted tool using budget models.
statsTools.analysisUniqueTracking: clawdboard is the only platform tracking real usage data across all {toolCount} tools side by side, providing a unique view of how developers actually use AI coding assistants in practice.
statsTools.analysisUniqueTrackingSingle: clawdboard is the only platform tracking real usage data across this tool, providing a unique view of how developers actually use AI coding assistants in practice.
statsTools.faqDescription: Common questions about AI coding tool usage data and comparisons.
statsTools.faqQ1: What AI coding tools does clawdboard track?
statsTools.faqA1: clawdboard tracks usage from {toolCount} AI coding {toolCount, plural, one {tool} other {tools}}: {toolSummary}. Each tool's usage is tracked separately, allowing side-by-side comparison of cost, token usage, and model preferences.
statsTools.faqQ2: Which AI coding tool is most popular?
statsTools.faqA2: Based on data from {totalUsers} developers, {topToolName} leads with {topToolCost} in estimated API cost ({topToolShare}% of total spend). Popularity is measured by estimated cost, which reflects total usage volume weighted by model pricing.
statsTools.faqQ3: How does clawdboard track usage across different tools?
statsTools.faqA3: The clawdboard CLI reads local log files from each supported tool on a developer's machine. {toolList} all maintain local usage logs. The CLI extracts token counts and model identifiers from these logs and syncs aggregate data to clawdboard. No code, prompts, or conversation content is collected.
statsTools.faqQ4: Can I use multiple AI coding tools with clawdboard?
statsTools.faqA4: Yes. If you use multiple supported tools, the clawdboard CLI will detect and sync usage from all installed tools. Your profile page shows a breakdown by tool, and the leaderboard aggregates your total spend across all tools.
statsTools.faqQ5: How are costs estimated for different tools?
statsTools.faqA5: All costs are estimated from token counts and published API pricing for each model. Usage is priced at the respective provider's rates (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.). These are API-equivalent estimates — most developers pay flat subscription fees.
statsTools.faqQ6: How often is tool comparison data updated?
statsTools.faqA6: Individual developers sync their usage every 2 hours by default. The aggregate statistics on this page are recalculated hourly. New tools are added to tracking as they gain community adoption.
statsTools.ctaDescription: Works with {toolList}. Free, open-source, takes 30 seconds.

statsModel.subtitle: {modelName} statistics
statsModel.heroDescription: Real usage data for {modelName} from {userCount} developers on clawdboard. {modelName} is {modelDescription}. All costs are estimated from token counts and published {provider} API pricing.
statsModel.overviewDescription: Aggregate {modelName} usage across all {userCount} developers who have used this model.
statsModel.trendsDescription: How much are developers spending on {modelName} each day? This chart shows the 7-day moving average of estimated daily cost and active user count. Spikes often correspond to new version releases or pricing changes.
statsModel.tokenBreakdownP1: Developers have consumed {totalTokens} tokens through {modelName}, split between {inputTokens} input tokens (prompts, context, files) and {outputTokens} output tokens (generated code, explanations, edits).
statsModel.tokenBreakdownCache: Prompt caching has processed {cacheCreationTokens} cache creation tokens and {cacheReadTokens} cache read tokens. Cache reads are significantly cheaper per token, reducing the effective cost for developers with repeated context.
statsModel.tokenRatioHighInput: The higher input-to-output ratio suggests developers send substantial context (files, documentation, error messages) along with their prompts.
statsModel.tokenRatioHighOutput: The higher output-to-input ratio suggests the model generates more content than it receives, typical for code generation and completion tasks.
statsModel.costAnalysisHeading: How Much Does {modelName} Cost for Coding?
statsModel.costAnalysisP1: Based on data from {userCount} developers, the average estimated {modelName} usage cost is {avgCost} per developer (all-time). The median is {medianCost}, reflecting the gap between occasional and heavy {modelName} users.
statsModel.costAnalysisP2: {modelName} accounts for {costShare}% of total community spend across all {totalModels} tracked models, ranking #{rank} by estimated cost.
statsModel.costAnalysisP2Flagship: As a flagship model, it commands a higher per-token price, which means it can dominate cost share even when other models see more total requests.
statsModel.costAnalysisP2Fast: As a fast, cost-efficient model, it processes more requests per dollar, so its cost share may underrepresent actual usage volume.
statsModel.costAnalysisP3: These are estimated API-equivalent costs, not actual bills. Most developers use {modelName} through subscription-based tools (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI) with flat monthly pricing. The estimated cost is useful for comparing usage intensity across models and developers.
statsModel.relatedModelsDescription: Compare {modelName} with other {provider} models tracked on clawdboard.
statsModel.faqDescription: Common questions about {modelName} usage, cost, and performance data.
statsModel.faqQ1: How much does {modelName} cost for coding?
statsModel.faqA1: Based on data from {userCount} developers on clawdboard, the average estimated {modelName} usage cost is {avgCost} per developer (all-time). The median is {medianCost}. These are estimated API-equivalent costs calculated from token counts and published {provider} pricing — most developers pay flat subscription fees, not per-token billing.
statsModel.faqQ2: How popular is {modelName} compared to other AI coding models?
statsModel.faqA2: {modelName} ranks #{rank} out of {totalModels} tracked models by total estimated cost, accounting for {costShare}% of all community spend on clawdboard. {userCount} developers have used {modelName}. Higher-tier models often dominate cost share even with fewer users due to higher per-token pricing.
statsModel.faqQ3: What is {modelName} used for in coding?
statsModel.faqA3: {modelName} is {modelDescription}. Developers use it through AI coding tools tracked by clawdboard — including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex CLI. The token ratio for {modelName} is {tokenRatioText}, indicating how developers interact with the model.
statsModel.faqQ4: Where does this {modelName} usage data come from?
statsModel.faqA4: All data comes from developers who voluntarily track their AI coding usage through clawdboard. The CLI reads local log files from supported tools on each developer's machine and extracts aggregate token counts and model identifiers. No code, prompts, or conversation content is collected — only token counts and estimated costs.
statsModel.faqA5: Individual developers sync their usage every 2 hours by default. The aggregate statistics on this page are recalculated hourly. {modelName} has been tracked on clawdboard since {trackedSince}.
statsModel.faqA5NoDate: Individual developers sync their usage every 2 hours by default. The aggregate statistics on this page are recalculated hourly. {modelName} has been tracked on clawdboard since it was first used by a community member.
statsModel.ctaDescription: See how your {modelName} usage compares. Free, open-source, takes 30 seconds.
